title: briza virens var. minor
intro: Briza virens var. minor, also known as lesser quaking grass, is a type of flowering plant in the family Poaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that can help to soothe and calm the skin. The plant has been reported to have several health benefits, including reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Lesser quaking grass can be prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is also used in traditional remedies for its ability to ease digestive issues and promote overall well-being.
